items.title: Warframe Guide: All Styanax Prime Relic Locations
items.summary: Get Styanax Prime fast with every Relic location, drop rate, and best farm mission. Complete your set without wasting a single run.
items.content:
Styanax has always been a sleeper pick in Warframe . Players who figured him out early knew the damage potential was real, but the broader community took its time catching up. Now that Styanax Prime has arrived alongside the Jade Shadows: Constellations update, there's no more reason to sleep on him. The Prime design is one of the sharpest the game has seen in a while, and farming his parts is more forgiving than most Primes thanks to how his drops are distributed across Relics.

There are four Relics to track down, each carrying a different component. Here's the full breakdown:

The distribution is worth paying attention to before you start farming. The Blueprint drops as a Common reward from Axi A21 , which sounds great until you remember that Axi Relics are the hardest tier to farm. The Neuroptics, on the other hand, is a Rare drop from Lith S18 , but Lith Relics are the easiest to accumulate in the game. So the tier difficulty and drop rarity are almost inverted here, which balances things out more than you'd expect.

Hepit in the Void is the go-to for Lith Relics, and it's not particularly close. It's a Capture mission with level 10 enemies, which means a fast Frame like Gauss, Volt, Titania, or Wukong can clear it in under a minute. Every completed run drops either a Lith Relic or Aya, with a 13.3 percent chance of landing the specific Lith S18 you need. The speed of the mission more than compensates for that percentage, since you can chain runs quickly.

tip Lith S18 carries the Rare Neuroptics, so plan to run Hepit more than you think you need to. Radiant Relics will push your odds up significantly when you're ready to crack them. Meso Y2 (Chassis, Uncommon) Io on Jupiter is the recommended Meso farm. It's a Defense mission where you only need to complete three waves before extracting, and rotation A guarantees a Meso Relic drop. Each cycle gives you a 12.5 percent chance of pulling the Meso Y2 specifically. If you want to double up and chase Meso and Neo Relics in the same mission, Ukko in the Void (Capture) can drop both Neo Y1 and Meso Y2, though the individual odds for each are roughly half compared to dedicated farms. The speed of Ukko runs can offset that if you're efficient.

Xini on Eris is the primary Neo Y1 source, offering a 10 percent drop chance per run. It's an Interception mission, which means it runs a bit longer than a Capture, but the node tends to have active players which makes it easier to fill a squad. The Ukko Capture option mentioned above applies here too if you prefer speed over certainty.

Apollo on Lua (Disruption) is the standard Axi farm. The catch with Disruption is that Axi Relics only appear on rotations B and C, so you need to defend all four conduits in the first round to progress through the rotations properly. If you want an alternative that covers multiple Relic tiers in one location, Kappa on Sedna (Disruption) drops Meso on rotation A, Neo on rotation B, and Axi on rotation C. It's not the fastest route for any single Relic, but it's worth knowing if you want variety in your farming sessions.

warning On Apollo (Lua), failing to defend conduits in round one means you won't reach rotation B or C, cutting off your Axi Relic drops entirely. Bring a capable loadout or coordinate with your squad. How do you open Styanax Prime Relics? Once you've stocked up on Relics, head to the Navigation screen and look for Void Fissure missions . Match the Fissure tier to the Relic you want to crack (Lith, Meso, Neo, or Axi), then complete the mission while collecting 10 Reactant from corrupted enemies. At the end, you'll pick one reward from the Relic.

The smarter play is to run these with a coordinated group. Jump into the Recruiting tab in chat and put together a squad where everyone runs the same Relic. At the end of a Fissure mission, you can select a reward from any squad member's Relic, not just your own. This means four players opening four Lith S18 Relics gives you four chances to see the Neuroptics instead of one.

important Make sure every squad member is running the same Relic at the same refinement level (Intact, Exceptional, Flawless, or Radiant). Mismatched refinements don't break the run, but Radiant Relics increase the odds of Rare drops, so coordinating refinement level is worth the extra Void Traces. Void Traces for upgrading Relics come from completing Void Fissure missions, so the farming loop feeds itself naturally. Prioritize spending Traces on Lith S18 Relics to push the Neuroptics from Rare odds to something more manageable.

Styanax Prime isn't the only reason to be farming right now. The update also introduced new content and questlines worth exploring. If you haven't started the main story content yet, check out the guide on how to start the Jade Shadows: Constellations quest to make sure you meet all the prerequisites before diving in.

Voruna Prime also launched recently, and if you're still chasing her components, the complete Voruna Prime Relics farming guide covers every drop location in detail. The two Prime farms can actually overlap at certain nodes, so running them in parallel is a reasonable approach if you need both.

tip Styanax Prime arrived after Voruna Prime despite Styanax releasing earlier in the base game. Voruna jumped the queue, but both are now available through standard Relic farming with no Prime Access purchase required. For a full breakdown of everything currently available in the game, the Warframe guides hub has you covered across builds, quests, and farming strategies.

items.title: Our Refreshed Refer a Friend Program
items.summary: Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below. Referral Code Changes You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform sav
items.content:
Make note, Tenno — Warframe’s Refer a Friend program update is now live! You can take a look at all the need-to-know details below.

Referral Code Changes

You can now create and send referral codes from any platform with a cross platform save enabled account! All you need to do is log into your account on Warframe.com, generate a referral code from the Refer a Friend page, then send that code to the friend you are trying to recruit. After your friend creates their Warframe account, they have seven days to qualify as your recruit. Your friend then inputs your referral code and links your accounts together by either:
  • Entering the referral code in-game by hitting "Communication > Friends > Refer Tenno".
  • Entering the referral code on the Account Management page.

Reward Changes

Both recruiters and recruits can earn Rewards through the new Refer a Friend system.

Recruiter Rewards

There are now four Reward tiers for recruiters to earn depending on how many referrals they send! Recruit 1 Tenno
  • 3x Forma
  • Orokin Catalyst
  • Orokin Reactor
  • Exilus Weapon Adapter
  • Exilus Warframe Adapter
  • Dream Catcher Honoria
Recruit 2 Tenno
  • Two Weapon Slots
Recruit 3 Tenno
  • Warframe Slot
Recruit 4 Tenno
  • Two Archwing Weapon Slots
  • Gravimag
Please note: Once your recruit completes "The Teacher" Quest, you will unlock your recruiter reward! The more Tenno you recruit, the more rewards you get! These Rewards are also retroactive for anyone that qualifies.

Recruit Rewards

After recruits successfully link their accounts, they will instantly gain the following in-game rewards:
  • 7-Day Affinity Booster
  • Classic Color Palette
  • Clan Key

Additional Rewards

Every time one of your recruits makes their first Platinum purchase, 10 percent of the value of Platinum they purchased will be automatically added to your account.

items.title: Warframe brings players a new sibling rivalry war in Jade Shadows Constellations
items.content:
Anyone who has a sibling knows how vicious sibling rivalries can get, but we’re going to be honest with you: Sirius and Orion in Warframe have it worse. For one thing, they’re literally trying to kill one another when you meet them in the game. For another, their parents are Jade and the Stalker. Also, they’re sort of not brothers because they’re actually the same person from parallel timelines , both of whom have traveled into the past to resolve their conflict.

“How does that work?” Gee, it’d be nice if there were a whole narrative quest detailing that! (Spoiler: There is, in fact, a whole narrative quest detailing that.) There are also new Railjack missions, the Steel Path difficulty for Railjack missions, new Augments for several Warframes, Sirius and Orion themselves as a Warframe, Styanax Prime, the new Incarnons, and quite a bit more in the latest major update. Check out the trailer below and catch up on all the patch details .

items.title: "Our players can swing different types of ways" - Warframe studio talks avoiding Protoframe burnout, and fending off fans' furious appetite for romance
items.summary: An interview with Digital Extremes community director and live ops lead Megan Everett protoframes.
items.content:
Image credit: Digital Extremes Feature by Connor Makar Staff Writer Published on June 18, 2026 5 comments Follow Warframe Warframe 's next update - Jade Shadows: Constellations - has finally arrived, bringing with it two new Protoframes: Ryoku and Vena. A relatively recent addition to the sci-fi shooter's playable repertoire, Protoframes - essentially human versions of the game's familiar Warframes - were a bold swing that won hearts and minds a few years back, and they've remained popular ever since.

When Protoframes were introduced in 2024's Warframe: 1999 expansion, they were accompanied by an in-game romance system to much acclaim. Once players got a taste, they've desired more ever since - but Jade Shadows: Constellations' new Protoframes are different.

"What's interesting about Ryoku and Vena is I'm sure people will feel a certain way about them not being romanceable, but they can't all be romanceable," community director and live ops lead Megan Everett told Eurogamer.

This time around, Warframe's new Protoframes play a mentor role for the stars of the update: Warframe brothers Orion and Sirius. Rather than attempting to woo Ryoku and Vena, players can side with one over the other, flying off for a space battle that concludes with a tense boss fight against your chosen foe. This is quite the drastic deviation from the norm with Protoframes, something Everett believes is necessary, even if she understands the demand for love.

"You have to branch out with Protoframes to make sure they still have that freshness, and dabble with what players like. But, I already see feedback about people wanting romance - I think everyone will always want romanceable Protoframes.

"I know if my favourite Warframe got a Protoframe, and I wasn't able to do anything like that, I'd be so upset. But if you play the Constellations quest, you'll understand why these characters are so linked to Sirius and Orion, and why they're not an option to be romanced."

It is worth noting that while a desire for romance is ever-present with Protoframes, it hasn't always been included with the introduction of new ones. During last year's Techrot Encore update, for instance, the game introduced four characters, each with their own narratives that meant romance didn't always make sense. Two of them were divorced, for example.

For Everett, while the presence of romanceable Protoframes has been good for the game, it cannot be so present that it begins to lose its lustre. "Our players love the romance systems - it shows our players can swing different types of ways," she explains. "They aren't all around the metagame; not just about the grind. They are open to different gameplay avenues. That's beneficial to us - the lore ones, romanceable ones, and now these mentors. We don't want to have this Protoframe burnout - if we did it for every one we released people would get annoyed."

There's also the challenge of throwing in an ever-growing collection of romanceable characters to an audience who may already feel happy with a pre-established in-game relationship:

"You can't make players care about a character so much, and then throw them another character they can date," Everett says. "When we released the Triad, we had to emphasise you could still date the Warframe 1999 characters, to make sure people wouldn't feel icky and gross. People got really attached to Protoframes, we're beyond appreciative. We just want to avoid frustration wherever possible."

This concern stretches beyond just the topic of romance, however. Too much of a good thing can have its downsides, and "Protoframe burnout" is an ever-present concern on the minds of Warframe's developers. In Everett's words, they have to "remain special".

"We do four new Warframes a year, plus Prime Warframes. Adding Protoframes into the mix in a consistent way would lead to burnout. So we have to pick-and-choose, and have a strategy for where they go. They have to make sense thematically, and fit into the narrative. It's not to say that Protoframes in the future won't be datable, or some won't have a divorce arc or whatever, but things must stay fresh."

items.title: Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology
items.summary: With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.
items.content:
Space Games Warframe's latest update is all about space combat, constellations, and cosmic mythology News By Luke Kemp last updated 18 June 2026 With its latest major update, Digital Extremes makes space for old mechanics and new ideas in the cosmos.

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

Click for next article (Image credit: Digital Extremes) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Defying recent live service trends in gaming, Warframe has been going strong for 13 years. It's one of the best space games out there, and it shows no signs of slowing down with the Jade Shadows: Constellations update.

The online sci-fi action RPG has evolved both technically and narratively over the years, changing from a game where the story has blossomed from background to the emotion-driven forefront of every major update. In 2024, Jade Shadows started the process of humanising Stalker - the player's long-term nemesis - by making him a father.

Jade Shadows: Constellations serves the dual purpose of continuing this story, and taking the first steps down the road of reinvigorating the game's space-based content.

Watch full video here: At the end of Jade Shadows, players chose one of two names for Stalker's son; Sirius, or Orion. The surprise hook for Constellations is that both came to be… in a way. Bursting onto the scene from parallel timelines, each has come to claim their father for their own. Sirius takes after their mother, while Orion takes after their father. Both are named after notable stellar phenomena: Sirius is the brightest star in the Earth's night sky, while Orion is one of the most prominent constellations visible from Earth (itself named after the legendary hunter in Greek mythology).

Stalker, when he appears during a mission, will relentlessly chase down the player until he or his target dies. What more appropriate name for the son that takes after him, therefore, than the constellation of the Hunter?

Fantasy game 'Crimson Desert' lets you stab your way up to space, showcasing a remarkably detailed cosmos 'SOL Shogunate' is a samurai space opera set on the moon, but with an aerospace engineer making sure it's 'getting the science right' Upcoming space & sci-fi games for 2026 & beyond: Saros, Warhammer 40K: Dawn of War 4, Halo: Campaign Evolved & more

"We thought one of the most impactful ways to end a quest would be letting the player name the child," explains Creative Director Rebecca Ford, "and we held that close to our hearts [...] And these names must come from the cosmos, they must come from space. Then we knew we were going to start going divergent paths pretty quickly."

Warframe's missions are spread across the Origin System, which looks very similar to our own solar system . Players jump between planets and moons with real-life equivalents such as Mars , Deimos , Neptune , Mercury , and Earth. The star Sirius and the constellation Orion also exist in the Warframe universe, and factor into the new quest; although exactly how is under wraps at time of writing.

Signup to our newsletter Follow us on Google Constellations is also being used as a springboard for refreshing the game's space battle Railjack content. "I absolutely felt that this update would not work without a Railjack component for the post quest content," explains Ford.

"It did not feel to me like you could do all of this grand space cosmos-like effort without giving players somewhere to use their Railjack. That is a very, very difficult choice in this line of work, where you need to be able to do things very well and very quickly.

"Railjack [is] such a complicated feature, like the code, and the streaming… everything about it is hard, it's making Warframe on hard mode," reveals Ford.

These are the 7 best sci-fi games I saw at Summer Game Fest 2026 Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' has lots of 'Mass Effect' DNA, but its developers are teasing a far more grounded sci-fi RPG "In this case we said all right, let's just commit, because we have help from our partners at Sumo, who are co-developing this update, and they love Railjack. So that helped! We just had the stubbornness to say it has to feel this big, it needs this; because it needs to acknowledge that that is how big of an event this quest is."

items.title: Consistency Leads to the Top: 'Warframe'
items.summary: ©INVEN📊Steam Check 13- Your daily Steam briefing, updated every day.
items.content:
Digital Extremes' action game 'Warframe' has climbed to 4th place in global popularity and 6th in Korea, following the release of its new narrative chapter, 'Jade Shadows.' The game is reaping the rewards of this update, with daily peak concurrent players hitting 100k for the first time since January. It has effectively surpassed Destiny 2, which had recently drawn attention as it approached its final update.

Service for over a decade since its 2013 launch, Warframe remains beloved for its high-speed gameplay, consistent updates, and low barrier to entry as a free-to-play (F2P) title. While its success has fluctuated with changes in key leadership, the game is widely praised for its non-P2W in-app purchase model, which minimizes player resentment, and its commitment to providing fresh experiences through continuous content additions.

With Destiny 2 announcing an end to its updates and the Borderlands series operating as a single-purchase franchise rather than a long-term live service, Warframe is effectively becoming the definitive title in the modern looter-shooter genre. By staying true to the core of the genre and the fundamentals of live service, it is solidifying its position as the genre leader.

MINTROCKET's 'DAVE THE DIVER' has released its first major story DLC since launch. In the globally released 'In the Jungle,' players leave the Blue Hole to resolve incidents in the jungle village of Utara. The expansion offers a fresh gameplay experience with over 10 hours of new story content, new systems, regions, a lake-based ecosystem, and the 'Bancho Grill' jungle restaurant.

'Backrooms,' which originated as an internet meme and found success as a film, has now landed on Steam as a co-op horror game. Developed by Ukrainian indie studio ShimStudio, 'Backrooms: Lost Runners' is a co-op survival horror game where a team trapped in endless corridors must explore abandoned camps and solve environmental puzzles to survive while evading entities that react to sound, light, and movement. To distinguish itself from simple walking simulators, it incorporates storylines, quests, resource management, character progression, bizarre anomalies, and exploration structures inspired by Metroidvania games.

PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS has returned to the #1 spot in both global and Korean popularity. KRAFTON launched a Steam publisher sale on the 18th, featuring discounts across its portfolio alongside a 'Double G-Coin' promotion for PUBG. The combination of the 4.21 update—which introduced the 'Ally Duo' system, Blue Zone adjustments, PUBG Fantasy, and a Black Market overhaul—and the large-scale publisher discount promotion has driven both renewed interest and increased purchasing demand.

K/DA and Taric - Coa, Haeun, Yeovlynn, Rakang, Bong

Creative use of ZEPETO - Abigelic Cosplay Spot

There's a funny silence that happens when you try to explain Dave the Diver to someone who hasn't played it. At least, that's the experience I had whenever my wife saw me playing the game on my Steam

They say no one can escape from the Violet Hold, but Hearthstone's rulebreakers are already preparing their jailbreak. In the new expansion, Escape from Violet Hold, launching on July 7th, 2026, a new wave of Legendary minions will arrive to shake up the rules of Hearthstone. Today, Inven Global introduces one of them: the new Neutral Legendary card, Inspector Murloc Holmes.

Lara Croft's new title started with something I didn't expect from a demo station: mist hanging low across the floor, the walls washed in green flora, the faint sound of running water out of view. I t

Today, it has become the industry standard to host online showcases to deliver new updates to gamers worldwide. From 'Summer Game Fest (SGF)', 'The Game Awards (TGA)', and 'Gamescom Opening Night Live

Phygital Contenders Astana 2026 has got underway, with clubs and athletes from around the world competing for the last qualification spots at this summer's Games of the Future. Running from June 10th

items.title: Warframe Guide: How to Get Sirius and Orion
items.summary: Get Sirius and Orion in Warframe fast. Complete the Constellations quest, farm Railjack missions, and spend Tokens at Pontis Tower.
items.content:
Sirius and Orion are unlike anything else currently in Warframe . Two brothers, one frame, sharing the same thread of fate and playable simultaneously. It's a concept Digital Extremes hasn't tried before, and the farming path to unlock them reflects that novelty. There are multiple routes depending on how much time or money you want to spend, and this guide covers every one of them.

Before any farming begins, you need to complete the Jade Shadows: Constellations quest. This is a direct sequel to the original Jade Shadows quest, so you must finish that first. Constellations is short, around 30 minutes from start to finish, and it rewards you with the main Sirius and Orion blueprint upon completion. No RNG, no grind, just finish the quest and the blueprint is yours.

The quest also unlocks Pontis Tower , a new hub on Uranus that becomes your main base of operations for everything Sirius and Orion related. Do not skip it.

Like every craftable Warframe, Sirius and Orion require three component blueprints in addition to the main blueprint:

Each blueprint drops from the Kuva Wytch and Scoria's Angel Railjack Skirmish missions in Uranus Proxima. These missions become accessible from the Railjack screen in Navigation after completing the Constellations quest. Every blueprint, including the main one, has a 14.29% drop chance from these missions, so expect some repetition before you collect the full set.

warning The frame takes 72 hours to craft once you have all four blueprints. Start the build as soon as you have everything to avoid waiting around at the finish line. How do Crimson and Emerald Tokens work? If the RNG isn't cooperating, the Pontis Tower vendor offers a direct purchase option. Completing Railjack missions in Uranus Proxima earns you Crimson Tokens and Emerald Tokens depending on which mission type you run. These tokens can be spent at the Pontis Tower vendor to buy blueprints outright.

tip Stick to one mission type (either Kuva Wytch or Scoria's Angel) rather than splitting your runs. Focusing on a single mission type lets you stack one token currency faster, which means you can buy missing blueprints without coming up short on both currencies. Token shop at Pontis Tower

There are three distinct paths, and the right one depends entirely on your situation.

Free-to-play farming route: Complete Constellations, grab the main blueprint, then run Uranus Proxima Railjack missions until you have all three component blueprints. Use Tokens to fill any gaps.

Platinum purchase: If you already have enough Platinum saved up, you can buy Sirius and Orion directly from the Arsenal section. You get an immediate Level 0 frame ready to build and mod.

Real-money bundle: The Jade Shadows: Constellations bundle is priced at $19.19 and includes the frame, their signature weapons, bonus Platinum, and additional extras. This bundle is expected to arrive on Steam with regional pricing shortly after launch.

tip The signature weapon for Sirius and Orion is a scythe. If you pick up the bundle, you get it immediately alongside the frame. If you're new to the game and unsure how to spend your Platinum or prioritize your progression, the Warframe beginner's guide covers exactly that.

After running through both Railjack mission types, a few things stand out as worth knowing before you start:

important You cannot access either Railjack mission until the Constellations quest is complete. There is no shortcut around this requirement. For more on what's currently available in the game, the Warframe: The Old Peace complete guide covers the latest update's new modes and endgame farming in full detail.

items.title: ‘Warframe’ “Jade Shadows: Constellations” Update Out Now
items.summary: Today, developer Digital Extremes launched their next major update & story chapter for their free-to-play looter shooter title 'Warframe', dubbed
items.content:
Mobile News Nintendo PC PlayStation Xbox Genghis Husameddin

Today, developer Digital Extremes launched their next major update & story chapter for their free-to-play looter shooter title ‘Warframe’, dubbed “Jade Shadows: Constellations”. Available now across PC, consoles, and mobile platforms, this update brings the game’s 65th Warframe: a “two playable characters in one” unit, Sirius and Orion, alongside new Protoframes. The latest narrative trailer also pays homage to old doods ahead of Father’s Day, as players Step back into the role of a newfound father, the Stalker, and bring an end to the fight between two versions of his son in an emotional Quest, inspired by adrenaline-pumping shounen anime, continuing the Jade Shadows story arc. We love you, dads!

“ Warframe has always been elevated by our biggest and boldest media inspirations while remaining grounded in everyday human experiences we can all relate to,” said Rebecca Ford , Warframe Creative Director. “ Constellations felt like a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to up our own ante, surprise players with the return to a beloved character story, and a playable Warframe unlike anything we’ve created before. It’s both a love letter to hard-working single dads and the dramatic rivalries found in our favorite anime. We’re only halfway through the year, and still have so much more left in store for the Warframe community, so tune into TennoCon in July for the latest surprises!”

Quell the quarrel between beings from competing timelines in the new Jade Shadows: Constellations Quest. Continue the story of Stalker and his child as two rival futures collide into the present. Witness the escalating conflict between Sirius and Orion, different versions of the same powerful entity fighting to preserve their existence at any cost. For the full list of Quest prerequisites, please see the Warframe Quest guide here .

Complete the new story Quest to unlock the latest Railjack spaceship missions in Uranus Proxima and seek out the latest Warframe weaponry:

Prepare Styanax Prime Access, arriving alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations :

Write a new page in the world of Warframe with new cosmetics, weapons, and ability retouches. Unleash a mythic new look to the scholar turned warrior, Dante , and his new Tytonis skin. Wield the infestation of Nidus , the adaptive scourge, once more to experiment with a fresh ability retouch, giving him more survivability and viability in combat. Step beyond familiar realities with the newest scythe melee weapon from either side, Pride, or Wrath, depending on what timeline it came from, Ryoku’s and Vena’s Protoframe hairstyles for the Operator/Drifter, four fresh Atragraph Mod designs to discover, and five new Incarnon weapons to master.

Warframe is available across PC via Warframe.com /Steam (Windows & Linux via Proton), Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch consoles, iOS, and Android devices. Full cross-save and crossplay are supported across all platforms.

Enjoying DayOne ? Do us a favour, and make us a ‘ Preferred Source on Google ‘ – that way, you’ll only be delivered more gaming coverage from our team.

Head over and let us know your thoughts in the Day O ne Forums , the perfect place to chat about games if you’re a little more old-school and prefer things in a longform format.

Prefer the chaos of live and fast moving conversation? Come and make some noise in the lively DayOne Community Discord Server instead. If you’re rocking a DayOne Membership, both places give special access for our supporters, and you’ll unlock a ton of exclusive content, just for you.

items.title: Styanax Prime Access
items.summary: Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his
items.content:
Glory calls for Styanax! The master of shield and spear has ascended into Prime Access, offering the steadfastness of an entire phalanx to those bold enough to wield him. Clad in Orokin Gold and outfitted with Exalted manifestations of his iconic Tharros and Axios, this unbreakable champion arrives alongside his signature Prime Weapons and exclusive Prime Accessories. Take advantage of instant access and acquire the Prime items you want by selecting from the Weapons, Prime, Complete and Accessories Packs, depending on your platform and preferences. You can also earn Styanax Prime and his signature Prime Weapons in-game by collecting and cracking Void Relics! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760260/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Weapons_Pack/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78326/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Prime/ https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/78308/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Complete/ https://store.steampowered.com/app/4760270/Warframe_Styanax_Prime_Access__Accessories_Pack/ Styanax Prime Access includes:

Styanax Prime

Shield of the innocent. Spear of justice. Styanax Prime holds the line in defiance of tyranny.

Afentis Prime

The gilded speargun of Styanax Prime shines like a beacon across the battlefield, invigorating allies and stunning enemies.

Athodai Prime

Unleash the song of the righteous warrior with this gilded pistol that goes into Overdrive on headshot kills, maximizing fire rate and ammo efficiency for a short time.

Exclusive Styanax Prime Glyphs

Styanax Prime Accessories:

  • Lanex Prime Syandana
  • Daurus Prime Armor
  • Tharros and Axios Prime Decoration
  • 90-Day Affinity Booster
  • 90-Day Resource Booster
 Note: You'll find the best value in the Styanax Prime Access Complete pack, which includes the Styanax Prime Warframe, his Prime Weapons and Accessories, as well as Platinum!

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Access Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Available Now
items.summary: ***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations! NEW QUEST The consequences continue in
items.content:
***Warning: Significant spoilers for the original Jade Shadows Quest follow!*** A rivalry for the ages has officially landed with the arrival of Warframe's latest update, Jade Shadows: Constellations!

NEW QUEST

The consequences continue in a new cinematic sequel Quest that picks up where 2024's Jade Shadows left off. Reassume control of the Stalker as he allies once more with the Tenno to navigate a conflict of celestial proportions. His dueling sons from parallel futures have come to the Origin System in search of their father, and will stop at nothing to claim him for their respective timelines. Only you can end their cycle of conflict and bring harmony to the Origin System!

NEW WARFRAME: SIRIUS & ORION

Dedicated warriors borne of relentless determination. Sirius & Orion comprise the very first dual Warframe, occupying the same slot in your Arsenal while jockeying for position on the battlefield as they rain cosmic destruction upon foes. With their signature Pride and Wrath Scythes you can use brutal heavy attacks to gain Overguard and increased Critical Chance.

Sirius Ability 1: Coronal Ejection

Hurl Sirius' Jade Light infused scythe, dealing Heat Damage while collecting any pickups in its path. Hold to swap to Orion and use Gravitic Slash.

Orion Ability 1: Gravitic Slash

Repel enemies with Orion’s sinister scythe, dealing Slash Damage while reducing shields and armor. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Coronal Ejection.

Sirius Ability 2: Jade Stars

Sirius conjures Jade Light motes that slowly regenerate over time. Attacking enemies launches the motes, dealing Heat Damage. Hold to swap to Orion and use Astral Shell.

Orion Ability 2: Astral Shell

Envelop Orion in an Astral Shell. Upon taking damage the shell becomes a decoy that draws fire until it's destroyed. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Jade Stars.

Sirius Ability 3: Light’s Sanctuary

Sirius creates a well of light that heals and revives allies, while reducing incoming damage. The well slowly grows in size and power. Hold to swap to Orion and use Event Horizon.

Orion Ability 3: Event Horizon

Orion forms a drifting black hole, trapping enemies within its gravity. Hitting the black hole with Gravitic Slash or Coronal Ejection will extend its duration and change its trajectory: Gravitic Slash will direct it away from Orion, and Coronal Ejection will pull it towards Sirius. Hold to swap to Sirius and use Light's Sanctuary.

Sirius & Orion Ability 4: Celestial Clash

Sirius & Orion take to the skies in a cosmic duel. Each attack consumes a Constellation Star to inflict colossal collateral Blast damage. Match the star's color to the Warframes color to gain increased Critical Chance.

NEW PROTOFRAMES: RYOKU AND VENA

Sirius & Orion haven't arrived on their own. Both have trained extensively under the guidance and mentorship of two equally ruthless fighters mired in an intense rivalry of their own. The Ash Protoframe, Ryoku, is a discerning assassin who delivers violence with a disturbing discipline that he has passed along to Sirius. His counterpart, the Garuda Protoframe Vena, indulges her bloodlust with a frenzied brutality befitting her "Wytch" moniker, which she has likewise instilled in Orion.

RETURN TO RAILJACK

Completing the Quest will unlock the Uranus Proxima which includes our most accessible Railjack Missions to date. Here there's no requirement to level up — or even own — a Railjack of your own! Simply enter the Pontis Tower hub, decide whether you'd prefer to fight for Ryoku or Vena, and board their stylized vessels to engage in some of Warframe's most epic space combat yet.

DANTE TYTONIS COLLECTION

Knowledge glides on dark wings with the Dante Tytonis Skin, Noctua Lophos and Gallistrix Warfan Skin. An appropriately avian-themed Collection for the venerable scribe Warframe.

NEW INCARNONS

Five new weapons have been altered by the infernal power of the Void for your destructive pleasure. Wield new Incarnon versions of the Ballistica (Crossbow), Destreza (Rapier), Obex (Sparring), Stug (Pistol), and Vectis (Sniper Rifle), each more indomitable than ever before.

QUALITY OF LIFE IMPROVEMENTS

Nidus Retouch

The avatar of the Infested, Nidus, is more pestilent than ever thanks to an Ability Retouch. Bestowed with significant health boosts and other improvements, this walking plague is both more accessible and enjoyable to wield on the battlefield.

Additional Improvements

As ever, we're improving the overall Warframe experience with several new additions and upgrades, including overhauled lighting in Corpus Ships, adding Damage Over Time (DOT) to enemy Health Bars, enhanced Junction Bosses, Camera offset options, Companion XP adjustments, four new Arcanes and more.

JADE SHADOWS: CONSTELLATIONS PACKS

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735390/Warframe_Constellations_Complete_Collection/ Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations!

items.title: How to get Sirius and Orion in Warframe
items.content:
Guide How to get Sirius and Orion in Warframe Embrace the hate.

Truly unique Warframes have arrived in Digital Extremes' free-to-play masterpiece, as Sirus and Orion make their way into the game. These are two Warframes that occupy the same thread of fate, so they can both be played at the same time. It's an interesting proposition, but how do you get your hands on these "brothers"?

To get the main blueprint for Sirius and Orion, you will need to complete the Jade Shadows: Constellations quest in the game. You must complete the first Jade Shadows quest before you can start it. The main blueprint for Sirius and Orion is rewarded upon completion of the quest.

The quest itself is fun, but pretty short, only taking me around 30 minutes or so to complete. I'm not going to go into any details here, as that would be pretty heavily spoiler territory, but it's a fun trip, with some cool moments.

The Sirius and Orion component blueprints are dropped from the new Kuva Wytch and Scoria's Angel Railjack missions that can be accessed from the Railjack screen in Navigation after you have finished the quest. The parts can drop as rewards from either mission, and can also be purchased at the new Pontis Tower hub on Uranaus for a new resource called Crimson and Emerald Talents that you also earn from the missions.

You can also purchase the main Sirius and Orion blueprint from Pontis Tower if you need to. The blueprint will cost 275 of the Emerald or Crimson Talents, while the parts will cost 90 Talents each. I would usually show a picture of the inventory here, but it contains a huge spoiler, so I don't want to ruin anything for anybody.

Be sure to check out the rest of our Warframe news and guides.

Shacknews staff does not use generative artificial intelligence (AI) in their content. Shacknews strictly prohibits the use of its content for AI training or to generate text, including text in the style or format used for this publication. Shacknews reserves all rights to this work.

Hailing from Ireland, Aidan has been conditioned by local weather conditions to survive hours at his PC grinding through whatever game is offering the lowest possible drop rates for loot. He thinks the easiest way to figure out what fans of games want to read is to just be a fan of games. You can normally find him logged into Warframe, Destiny, or a gacha game. You can reach out to him on X @scannerbarkly .

Aidan O'Brien posted a new article, How to get Sirius and Orion in Warframe

items.title: All Styanax Prime Relics in Warframe
items.content:
Guide All Styanax Prime Relics in Warframe One man army.

Warframe's Styanax has been a "if you know, you know" option for many players over the years. Incredible damage output makes him a favorite among those who love to delete enemies from the servers. Now, Styanax Prime has arrived, bringing easily one of the best-looking Prime designs that we have ever seen to the game.

The Relics you will need to farm for Styanax Prime are the Axi A21, Lih S18, Meso Y2, and Neo Y1. Finding them will allow you to farm the following parts:

Quick Relic farming is vital to getting everything you need as quickly as you can, so the following missions are great.

Once you have the Relics you need, you then need to open them. This can be done by going to the Navigation screen and selecting the Void Fissure missions. Select the one that matches your desired type, then dive in and collect ten pieces of reactant from golden enemies to crack it open. At the end of the mission, you will get a randomly selected reward from the Relic.

If you want better odds of getting the part you want, then jump into the Recruiting tab in the chat and put together a group of people who are interested in opening the same Relic. You can pick rewards from a teammate’s Relic without interfering with their options, so feel free to grab something from them if it’s what you are looking for. Just make sure you are all running the same Relic at the same level (Intact, Radiant, Exceptional, or Flawless). You can alter those levels using Void Traces earned in the Void Fissure missions.

And there you go, everthing you need to know to get your hands on the Styanax Prime Relics. Be sure to check out the rest of our Warframe news and guides.

items.title: Warframe Creative Director Says New Characters Were Inspired by Super Smash Bros.
items.summary: Warframe's Rebecca Ford reveals how Super Smash Bros. inspired the new 2-in-1 characters in Jade Shadows, plus an exclusive look at Ryuko’s Railjack.
items.content:
Jade Shadows: Constellations, the next Narrative Chapter of Warframe, is being released on June 17 and, according to the development team, it’s unlike anything Warframe players have seen before. In the new update, the 65th Warframe is actually two Warframes in one. I spoke exclusively with Rebecca Ford, Warframe’s Creative Director, ahead of the new chapter’s launch to talk about the mechanics of switching between characters, Ryoku’s Railjack – which we’re exclusively revealing, the long-awaited Stug Incarnon, and how the new update was partially inspired by Super Smash Bros.

“There is a Super Smash Bros. reference for all of our choices because originally we did not know what to do with Sirius and Orion,” Ford says, referring to the two characters who share a Frame in Jade Shadows: Constellations. “I was like, okay, this is going to kind of be like having Pit or Dark Pit or Samus and Dark Samus in the Smash roster. They're two different characters, but they're a very similar flavor.

“I saw a lot of people saying, ‘This is Ice Climbers,’ which is great. We want Cain and Abel. We want biblical and heavenly levels of cosmic violence. [Design Director] Pablo [Alonso] just went for it and the only thing I said [was] ‘They really need to feel cosmic and planetary.’”

In the game, Sirius is capable of throwing his signature scythe like a “boomerang capable of picking up orbs” while Orion swings “a massive horizontal slash that significantly reduces enemy defenses.” Ford explained exactly how these two characters are able to occupy the same Frame.

“Imagine you enter a Warframe mission and you have an empty constellation,” Ford says. “It's an empty slot with all these stars that you get to fill in. Every time you actively choose to swap, you're starting to build out the constellation. Once it's built, you can do the most effective cosmic clash, which is the brothers fighting. So [when] you enter your mission, you can start alternating between powers, giving them each control. Every time you swap, you populate your constellation with a star.

“Once your constellation is complete, you can spend them beat by beat. And that is very much like a ‘Simon Says' game, but it happens in the sky. It's huge. It takes over the map. You are basically bringing the battle of the gods to whatever mission you're playing."

Speaking of replayability, Ford says that although it’s not a launch feature, the team at Digital Extremes will be adding an option to restart the quest and tweak actions and decisions to unlock different parts of the game.

“There's different dialogue, different cinematics, some of the VO is different, and some of the moments are different. It's not just plot, it's also story and theme. So when and if you get to the end of the quest, which will be about 35 minutes, you will find a world state that is only possible because of the broader themes that this quest explores.

“Your choices matter, but like all things Warframe, we want you to experience everything. You'll need to go in, make a choice, replay the quest, and then you can see the other side. But it's sort of the difference between strawberry and chocolate and not the difference between a hotdog and a hamburger. It's flavor.”

Speaking of flavor, one of the most anticipated additions to Jade Shadows: Constellations is the inclusion of the Incarnon form of the infamously weak community-meme weapon, the Stug.

“The name alone is kind of absurd,” Ford says. “The way it works, equally absurd. We only made five Incarnons this year so far. So giving it one of those five spots is a very intentional choice of saying, ‘We're committing to this bit and you're going to like it. And if you like the Stug, this is your day.’

“It's like a farmer deciding to rotate their crops in the field. If you're overdrawing from the same fantasy every update, your crops will fail to thrive. So in this case, it's very much a crop rotation of us picking fresh things to put some fertilizer on and say, ‘Okay, this is the time of this Stug and we know it's a ridiculous time, but by God, you can't do it so we'll do it for you.’”

As part of my conversation with Ford, I got a first look at Ryoku’s Railjack. The exclusive image, which you can see above, shows what is – according to Ford – more than just a ship.

“This is [Ryoku’s] headquarters. This is going to be the scene, the setting of one of the most environmental boss fights you'll ever see in Warframe. [There will be] brand-new traps and puzzles and obstacles. The team that worked on the interior and the exterior of this basically committed all the way. The ship itself is basically a fight. So it’s very, very fun.”

items.title: Warframe Jade Shadows: Constellations Exact Release Date & Start Times
items.summary: Warframe Jade Shadows: Constellations releases June 17. Get the exact times, new Warframe details, and quest breakdown. Read the full guide.
items.content:
Warframe is getting its next major update on June 17, 2026 , and it's a big one. Jade Shadows: Constellations brings a new cinematic quest that continues the Stalker's story, two new Warframes that operate as a dual pair, and the usual wave of free content that Digital Extremes has made standard for major drops. Here's everything confirmed so far, including the exact release times by region.

Digital Extremes announced the June 17 release date during an earlier Devstream, and based on how every major update including The Old Peace has launched, expect the servers to go live at 10am CT . The full regional breakdown:

The headline feature is a fresh main quest that picks up directly after the events of Jade Shadows. You'll be working alongside Ryoku and Vena , but the quest doesn't let you ride both sides at once. You have to pick one of them and then raid the other's ship. That kind of binary choice is rare for Warframe quests, and it should give the story some actual tension.

The quest runs in both solo and co-op , so you can experience it however you prefer. The full scope of the quest won't be clear until the update is live, but the Stalker's story continuing here is the thread most long-term players have been waiting on.

The update introduces two new Warframes: Sirius and Orion . What makes them different from every other frame in the game is their dual setup. You can switch between them mid-mission, and each brings a distinct playstyle. The balance between the two is entirely up to you, which opens up a lot of build flexibility depending on what the mission demands.

This is the kind of mechanic that could change how you approach loadout planning. Whether you lean into one frame heavily or keep both tuned for different situations is a genuine decision rather than an obvious right answer.

important Both Sirius and Orion are part of the free update, but microtransactions will be available for immediate access and additional benefits. If you want to earn them through gameplay, you can do that without spending anything. Sirius and Orion ability layout

Yes. Like every major Warframe update, Jade Shadows: Constellations is free to download and play. Microtransactions exist for players who want to skip the grind for the new frames or pick up cosmetic extras, but nothing is paywalled in terms of core content.

If you're newer to the game and want to get up to speed before the update lands, the Warframe beginner's guide covers which frames to prioritize, how to spend Platinum wisely, and how to move through the Star Chart efficiently.

The source material doesn't specify exact quest prerequisites, so treat this section as a flag to check in-game once the update goes live. Warframe cinematic quests typically require completing earlier story content first, and given that Constellations follows directly from Jade Shadows , having that quest completed beforehand is the safe assumption.

This update lands in a busy stretch for the game. The Warframe 13 Year Anniversary event has been running with 8 weeks of free Dex rewards and booster weekends, and Constellations drops right into that window. If you've been logging in for the anniversary content, you're already in a good position to hit the new update fresh.

For players who want to stay across everything happening in the game right now, the full Warframe guides collection has dedicated pages for The Old Peace, Operation Atramentum, Voruna Prime Access, and active promo codes.

items.title: Design Mockument: What would a hypothetical Warframe 2 look like?
items.content:
This one has a weird genesis, and I’m going to ask you to roll with me on it because it starts with Guild Wars 3 .

Now that we actually have an announcement, I am relatively sure I’m not exactly alone in expecting GW3 to be one of the next big MMORPGs just like its predecessor. That got me thinking about the Big Five , which arguably at this point should be the Big Four, and which of the games in there could really roll with a sequel to shake things up. Of course, nothing says that GW3 needs to knock its predecessor out of the running, but that also makes me think that the Big Five had some unexpected entries in there alongside even existing as a thing.

So let’s say we’re in a new era, and let’s say GW3 is the vanguard of that new era. That made me start thinking about what theoretical new game could pop up that would be surprising and yet a welcome addition… and herein lies Warframe hypothetically getting a sequel. Let’s say that’s the big announcement at this year’s TennoCon (which I severely doubt). So what would this Warframe 2 even look like in the first place? What would we want it to look like?

Let’s start with something as a foundational concept: Warframe 2 is an MMORPG through and through. Warframe is an MMO that frequently flirts with being an MMORPG, so let’s go all the way and just transfer it. And the best thing is that you don’t even need to worry too much about the setting because in Warframe we already have Eternalism paving the way for us. Which means we’re into a parallel universe, one wherein the war in Tau was won far more decisively, even though the Orokin still fell. The Tenno have awakened once more… in a distant system. Not the Origin system, but somewhere else, guided by Cephalon Margulis.

Fortunately for us, a great deal has changed during the time when the Tenno slept during our long voyage. One of those changes has been a complete revision of how Warframes as a concept work. They have been distilled and improved, turned into a more perfect vessel… because no longer are they based on heroes and identities from the past, but the Tenno themselves.

To start, character creation is now something you start with, creating both your personal Warframe and your Operator from the start. Your Warframe is based broadly on a few basic styles, with numerous morphing changes that you can apply, but frames no longer have distinct identities from one another as they do in the original. A wide array of different skins and helmets from the original are available to define your look right from the beginning.

Abilities, however, are another matter. Each frame has a Core, and each Core is based around a different basic concept. There are six Cores available, and within each Core you can define abilities and modify them based on the five Tennokai schools. In broader strokes, these Cores are based on well-known Warframes from the original game, with a variable cost for abilities, stat modifications, and other alterations based on the abilities you select. So you can mix and match multiple abilities, but if you pick a bunch of really strong abilities and mash them together, you’re going to have a more fragile frame on the whole.

Of course, you can also improve these limits by – yes – polarizing your frame and resetting your abilities back down to a lower level. It’s a combination of several systems into one, in other word, with the net result being you create a custom Warframe tuned the way that you want it.

Weapons are a bit different. Rather than having a primary, secondary, and melee weapon you can equip, you can wield two weapons at a time. For certain weapons, you can wield them simultaneously, like having a glaive in one hand and a pistol in the other, or a pistol or sword in each hand. Other weapons you have to manually swap between, like a rifle and a katana. You can save multiple weapon loadouts and swap between them at weapon lockers, which can sometimes be found mid-mission and are scattered throughout open maps.

Oh, did I not mention that? Yes, this hypothetical game has a lot of open-world map areas. In fact, a lot of the game is actually about traversal. It’s here that the Operator gets to shine; rather than being a specialized ability magnet, Operators are the default in settlements and in the open world, which is much more MMO-styled than even the shared maps existing in the game now. Vehicles are also customized for Operators, allowing you to use different traversal methods to explore the area.

So what about the game’s actual gameplay content? In comes in a few different forms; randomized tilesets with different objectives are available in the style of radio missions from City of Heroes , as well as larger cinematic questlines akin to the current game. More often than not, however, a lot of your missions are more abstract and explorative than that. You need to get certain resources, for example; your objective is to first find where you can get them and how, then farm for them.

Some resources are only found inside of enemy strongholds, and there are certainly several of those. The enemies of the Origin System are not found out here, but the Sentients are, as is a strange form of the Infestation crawling across the initial world. In addition, there are a couple of new factions to contend with, one of which seems to have been pulled from the timeline of the original game, another featuring purely mechanical enemies that nevertheless pose a serious threat.

The main goal of the game overall is to promote a slower and more contemplative vibe than the original while still maintaining the parts of the original that work well. The shooting gameplay of Warframe is exceptionally strong and satisfying, but this provides an opportunity to still get that during missions while also being more willing to slow down. There’s space to expand into some of the other things that the game has done, as well, from railjack exploration to archwings… or to never touch on those again and go into a completely different direction.

Of course, the odds of anything like this happening seem fairly low to me; one of the things that I’m struck by repeatedly is how well Warframe hides its age, and the fact of the matter is that it feels like exactly the experience it wants to be. Trying to make it into more of an MMORPG has actually generally been a thing that didn’t work all that well. Maybe it’s not worth trying, outside of Soulframe , and that’s a different experience to boot.

items.title: Equinox Prime and Wukong Prime Enter Prime Resurgence
items.summary: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium
items.content: Equinox Prime From the far edge of fate between day and night, she rises. Equinox Prime harnesses the power of duality to support others — offensively in her Day form and defensively with her Night form. For the heart that seeks equilibrium, there’s arguably no better fit than Equinox Prime. Wukong Prime Trickery’s simian avatar possesses strength equal only to his mischievous nature. Survivability is what Wukong Prime is all about, with several of his Abilities allowing him to cheat death or create celestial twins that draw attention from enemies. If keeping foes on their toes is your thing, Wukong Prime should do the trick.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Packs Coming Soon
items.summary: Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more
items.content:
Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations launching alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more. Jade Shadows: Constellations launches June 17 on all platforms. Now you can take these dueling reflections home by purchasing one of our latest Supporter Packs — either with your local currency from Warframe.com or the in-game Market for Platinum!

Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle

What might have been becomes reality with Sirius & Orion. Assume the full might of dueling dark futures and follow the stars to constellations of combined power. Featuring:
  • Sirius & Orion Warframe*
  • Pride (Scythe Weapon)
  • Wrath (Scythe Weapon)
  • Constellations Fusion Display
  • 190 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)
*Please note that Sirius & Orion are a single Warframe and not two separate Warframes.

Constellations Gemini Collection

Fight an eternal battle for the future with Vena, the blood-loving fanatic, and Ryoku, the cool and calculating killer. Two bitter rivals brought together in this collection of Protoframe Gemini Skins. Each skin can switch between Warframe and Protoframe appearances by activating the corresponding Gemini Emote. Featuring:
  • Ryoku Gemini Skin
  • Ryoku Gemini Emote
  • Vena Gemini Skin
  • Vena Gemini Emote
  • 375 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)

Dante Tytonis Collection

Choose the path of wisdom with the Dante Tytonis Collection. His dark wings harbor secret knowledge and arcane power. Featuring:
  • Dante Tytonis Skin
  • Noctua Lophos (Dante's Exalted Tome)
  • Gallistrix Warfan Skin

Constellations Complete Collection

Confront the legacy of a doomed future. Is there room for harmony in the present? Featuring:
  • Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle
  • Constellations Gemini Collection

Razor's Edge Salon Pack

The future might be doomed, but style doesn’t have to be with this collection featuring Ryoku and Vena’s hairstyles for the Operator and Drifter. Find these bundles in the in-game Market with full regional pricing when Jade Shadows: Constellations launches on June 17!

items.title: Devstream 196 Overview
items.summary: Our latest Devstream revealed the June 17 release date for Jade Shadows: Constellations! We dove deep into the details surrounding our first-ever dual Warframe, Sirius & Orion, as well as the unique new combat experience offered by their st
items.content:
Our latest Devstream revealed the June 17 release date for Jade Shadows: Constellations!

We dove deep into the details surrounding our first-ever dual Warframe, Sirius & Orion, as well as the unique new combat experience offered by their star-powered Abilities and signature Pride and Wrath weapons. We also took to the stars with Ryoku and Vena for a closer look at the latest Railjack Missions in the new Uranus Proxima. Somehow along the way we found time to discuss Styanax Prime, Mesa Heirloom, Summer Game Fest and, of course, TennoCon 2026. Check out our full Overview for all the details you may have missed.
